LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE OCT-23-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS BARBARA JOHNCK AND SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. HEARING ON DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE ARE CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 6, 2020 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302, DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO ABIDE BY THE PAGE LIMIT FOR AN OPPOSITION BRIEF. (CRC 1113(D).) PLAINTIFF SHALL FILE A COMPLIANT OPPOSITION BY OCTOBER 28, AND DEFENDANTS MAY FILE A REPLY THERETO BY NOVEMBER 2. BRIEFS ARE TO EXPLAIN THE APPARENT INCONSISTENCY BETWEEN THE NUMBERING OF CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE DEMURRER AND IN THE COMPLAINT THAT WAS PROVIDED BY PLAINTIFF. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF ROZENA M. HARTENS COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100114230) FILED BY DEFENDANT JOHNCK, BARBARA , AN INDIVIDUAL SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BARBARA JOHNCK AND SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT, IN PLAINTIFF'S PRESENCE, "A BLACK FEMALE EMPLOYEE SHARED THAT SHE SPOKE ENGLISH AND FRENCH, JOHNCK LAUGHED AT HER AND SAID, 'YOU FORGOT EBONICS.'" (ID. AT PARA. 35.) THIS IS A SUFFICIENT BASIS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AT THE PLEADING STAGE. FURTHER, AS RULED ON DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER, A HARASSMENT CLAIM IS ADEQUATELY PLED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-06-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-09-2020 Off Calendar

Defendants San Francisco Unified School District And Barbara Johncks Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Oriet Cohen-Suppoole of the law offices of Wright & Supple LLP (email: oriet@wrightandsupple.com) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTs BARBARA JOHNCK and SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint is DENIED. The complaint alleges that, in plaintiff's presence, "a Black female employee shared that she spoke English and French, Johnck laughed at her and said, 'You forgot Ebonics.'" (Id. at para. 35.) This is a sufficient basis for punitive damages at the pleading stage. Further, as ruled on defendants' demurrer, a harassment claim is adequately pled. Written order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Oriet Cohen-Suppoole of the law offices of Wright & Supple LLP (email: oriet@wrightandsupple.com) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTs BARBARA JOHNCK and SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRERS to COMPLAINT are OVERRULED.

Harassment (Govt. Code 12940(j)(1)): Defendants assert that plaintiff "fails to allege a 'concerted pattern' of harassment" and that "the alleged harassment consists of non-actionable personnel management actions." (Not. 1:9-12.) Plaintiff points to her complaint's dozen examples of harassment - enough to plead a pattern. (Id. at 5:24-21:6.) In arguing that their conduct was "non-actionable," defendants raise factual disputes. But a demurrer admits the truth of all facts alleged in a complaint no matter how unlikely. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.)

Investigation (Govt. Code 12940(j&k)): Defendants argue that plaintiff "fails to allege sufficient facts showing that she complained her rights had been violated." (Not. 1:13-16.) However, plaintiff pleads several complaints to her SFUSD supervisors. (Cmplt. para. 40, 41, 45, 47, 48.)

Family Rights (Govt. Code 12945.2(l)(1)): Defendants say SFUSD "did not interfere with" plaintiff's "CFRA leave" and "she was no longer entitled to reinstatement under CFRA." (Not. 1:17-20.) Again, defendants war with the complaint's allegations, which must be accepted as true. (Del E. Webb, 123 Cal.App.3d at 604.)

Emotional Distress: Johnck can be sued for intentional infliction of emotional distress that was outside the scope the scope of her employment, as plaintiff asserts here. (Hoff v. Vacaville Unified School Dist. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 925, 932.) The complaint's allegations against Johnck are adequate in this regard.

Retaliation (Govt. Code 12940(h&m): The complaint adequately pleads retaliation for engaging in protected activity. (Id. at para. 213-228.) Written order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Continued To Dec-09-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants San Francisco Unified School District And Barbara Johncks Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-23-2020 Continued To Nov-06-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS BARBARA JOHNCK AND SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Hearing on defendants' demurrer and motion to strike are continued to November 6, 2020 at 9:30 AM in Department 302, due to plaintiff's failure to abide by the page limit for an opposition brief. (CRC 1113(d).) Plaintiff shall file a compliant opposition by October 28, and defendants may file a reply thereto by November 2. Briefs are to explain the apparent inconsistency between the numbering of causes of action in the demurrer and in the complaint that was provided by plaintiff.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
